methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $82M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Firestorm Labs is a Series A–stage Defense & Security company founded by Dan Magy that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private firms and 2 public companies operating in the same sector, including peers such as Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses total capital raised ($60M) as a proxy baseline, adjusted to $82M, then applies a typical Series A–to–next–stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x, yielding an implied valuation of $164M. Peer-Median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparable companies—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M. Both methods proved applicable to this stage and sector profile.

Synthesizing these approaches yields a triangulated range of $139M (low) to $345M (high), with a median estimate of $232M. The Stage-Progression method anchors the lower bound, while Peer-Median drives the ceiling. Key uncertainties include the relative maturity and revenue traction of individual peer companies, variations in customer concentration within the Defense & Security sector, and the extent to which capital efficiency differs between Firestorm Labs and its comparables. Additional due diligence on customer composition and contract backlog would refine this range.
